records.ety: [AS. findan; akin to D. vinden, OS. & OHG. findan, G. finden, Dan. finde, icel. & Sw. finna, Goth. finþan; and perh. to L. petere to seek, Gr. pi`ptein to fall, Skr. pat to fall, fly, E. petition.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Found (found); p. pr. & vb. n. Finding.]

defs: To meet with, or light upon, accidentally; to gain the first sight or knowledge of, as of something new, or unknown; hence, to fall in with, as a person.
quotes: Searching the window for a flint, I found This paper, thus sealed up.
In woods and forests thou art found.

defs: To learn by experience or trial; to perceive; to experience; to discover by the intellect or the feelings; to detect; to feel.
quotes: The torrid zone is now found habitable.

defs: To come upon by seeking; as, to find something lost.
To discover by sounding; as, to find bottom.
To discover by study or experiment direct to an object or end; as, water is found to be a compound substance.
To gain, as the object of desire or effort; as, to find leisure; to find means.
To attain to; to arrive at; to acquire.
quotes: Every mountain now hath found a tongue.

defs: To provide for; to supply; to furnish; as, to find food for workemen; he finds his nephew in money.
quotes: Nothing a day and find yourself.

defs: To arrive at, as a conclusion; to determine as true; to establish; as, to find a verdict; to find a true bill (of indictment) against an accused person.
quotes: To find his title with some shows of truth.

defs: To determine an issue of fact, and to declare such a determination to a court; as, the jury find for the plaintiff.
Anything found; a discovery of anything valuable; especially, a deposit, discovered by archæologists, of objects of prehistoric or unknown origin.
